name: Femasys — OBGYN Office-Based Fertility (FemaSeed, Michigan)
description: Michigan office-based OBGYN / women's health practices, sourced from MedScout ovulation-induction prescriber data so every practice is ALREADY a confirmed prescriber. That prescriber volume is the seed (why they are on the list), NOT a website signal — this ICP never reads prescriber counts. It scores what the practice's PUBLIC WEBSITE confirms on top of that seed: fertility services, IUI specifically, and cash-pay readiness. Preserves v9 intent: the net is wide (any fertility activity qualifies for fit, nothing is a hard gate), readiness reinforces confidence only, and the screening burden moves onto exclusions. IVF/REI fertility centers and hospital / health-system-affiliated practices are removed. FemVue practices are existing Femasys relationships and the strongest warm leads.
scoring_notes: v9-MI (client-confirmed v9 logic refined with the Michigan asks). Design: fit is soft (no required_for_bullseye gate); the screening burden sits on exclusions, which are load-bearing. IUI (S-MI-001) is a DISTINCT, heaviest signal per the client's explicit Michigan call-out — never folded into the broad fertility signal. Fertility services (S-MI-002) is a single phrase-bound catch-all that collapses the v9 medicated-cycle / infertility-eval / fertility-consult signals, since the list is already a confirmed-prescriber seed and the site only needs to confirm fertility activity. All FIT prompts use strict phrase-binding so generic OBGYN copy (bare 'consultation', 'evaluation', 'treatment', 'testing') does not false-positive. FemVue is a warm-lead accelerator (boost + floor_tier Contender), deliberately NOT large enough to be required for Bullseye. READINESS (cash-pay / patient financing, reinforced by elective-aesthetic cash-pay) reflects adoption velocity and reinforces confidence only — it can never qualify a practice alone. EXCLUSIONS: IVF/REI fertility center (exclude_if_yes) plus the rei_on_staff / hospital_owned / health_system_affiliated structural gates in run_config drop out-of-profile practices. max_positive = 82 (IUI 28 + fertility 18 + monitoring 6 + FemVue 14 + cash-pay 16). bullseye_min_score is 80 (set in run_config): the score blend caps near 96 for a perfect record, so 80 leaves Bullseye reachable for strong fertility fit with or without FemVue. INCOME OVERLAY (median household income by ZIP via ACS B19013) is a separate post-scoring enrichment, NOT an ICP signal, and is not implemented here.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly offer IUI as a service? Answer yes ONLY when the page contains the exact term 'IUI' or the full phrase 'intrauterine insemination'. Do NOT infer from generic words like 'fertility', 'consultation', 'treatment', or 'family planning' — the specific term must appear verbatim.
signals.note: Core fit, heaviest signal. The client called IUI out specifically for Michigan: a listed in-office IUI service line is the clearest match for the office-based fertility work FemaSeed upgrades. Kept DISTINCT from the broad fertility-services signal, not folded in. Phrase-bound so it does not fire on generic fertility copy.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer fertility or infertility services? Answer yes ONLY when one of these exact phrases appears: 'infertility evaluation', 'infertility workup', 'fertility consultation', 'fertility treatment', 'fertility testing', 'ovulation induction', or 'infertility diagnosis'. Do NOT match bare standalone words such as 'consultation', 'evaluation', 'treatment', 'testing', or 'diagnosis' — those false-positive on nearly every OBGYN site; the full fertility-bound phrase must be present.
signals.note: Broad fertility-activity fit. Collapses the v9 medicated-cycle / infertility-eval / fertility-consult signals into one phrase-bound catch-all, because the Michigan list is already a confirmed-prescriber seed and the website only needs to confirm fertility activity on top of that. Strict phrase-binding keeps generic 'consultation/evaluation' copy out.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice perform fertility cycle monitoring by ultrasound? Answer yes ONLY when one of these exact phrases appears: 'follicle monitoring', 'cycle monitoring', 'monitoring ultrasound', or 'transvaginal ultrasound'. Do NOT match the bare word 'ultrasound' or 'monitoring' alone.
signals.note: A plus, not a gate. Cycle-monitoring ultrasound reinforces that fertility treatment happens in-office and adds confidence, but its absence is not disqualifying, so it carries a modest fit weight and no penalty when not found.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice mention FemVue anywhere on the site? Answer yes ONLY when the exact product name 'FemVue' appears.
signals.note: Warm-lead accelerator, not a gate. FemVue is Femasys's own product; a practice already using it is an existing relationship and the lowest-friction FemaSeed adoption path. It carries a meaningful fit boost plus floor_tier (a confirmed FemVue practice never falls below Contender), but it is deliberately NOT large enough to be required for Bullseye: strong fertility fit reaches Bullseye on its own merits, and FemVue accelerates rather than gatekeeps.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website show explicit evidence of cash-pay / self-pay capability or third-party patient financing? Answer yes when any of these exact terms is present: 'CareCredit', 'FSA', 'HSA', or 'patient financing'. Answer yes only when such language is explicitly present on the site.
signals.note: Readiness target. Direct-billing / financing infrastructure predicts adoption velocity for a patient-pay device workflow. Reinforced by the elective-aesthetic signal (S-MI-006) when explicit cash-pay language is absent but elective cash-pay services are present. Readiness reinforces confidence only and never qualifies a practice on its own.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice offer elective, aesthetic, or cosmetic services that are typically elective, non-covered, or patient-pay in routine commercial practice? Answer yes when one or more of these is explicitly named: 'aesthetics', a 'MedSpa' / 'med spa'; 'Botox'; 'body contouring'; 'CoolSculpting'; 'laser hair removal'; 'IV therapy'; weight-loss injectables ('GLP-1', 'semaglutide'); or 'labiaplasty'. Answer yes only when one or more such services are explicitly named on the site.
signals.note: Readiness reinforcer. Elective aesthetic / cash-pay services are patient-pay by definition, so their presence implies direct-billing infrastructure even when no explicit cash-pay language appears. Weight 0: it contributes only by inferring the cash-pay readiness target (S-MI-005), never independently inflating fit.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice operate as, or hold itself out as, an IVF or reproductive-endocrinology fertility center? Answer yes when any of these appears: 'IVF', 'in vitro fertilization', 'egg retrieval', 'embryo transfer', 'reproductive endocrinology', 'reproductive endocrinologist', 'REI', or the practice naming itself a 'fertility center'.
signals.note: Exclusion. A confirmed yes means a specialized fertility center that routes patients to in-house ART and male-factor pathways, not the office-based OBGYN profile FemaSeed upgrades. The NPI taxonomy structural gate (rei_on_staff) pre-filters most of these before crawl; this signal catches any that reach the LLM pass. Hospital / health-system / academic practices are removed by the structural exclusion rules in run_config, not by a textual signal.
